sections.title: E → IE Stem Changes
sections.explanation: Some Spanish verbs change the stressed vowel 'e' in their stem to 'ie' when that syllable is stressed. This happens in all forms EXCEPT nosotros and vosotros, creating what is called the 'boot verb' pattern (imagine drawing a boot shape around the affected forms). Key verbs: querer (to want/love) → quiero, quieres, quiere, queremos, queréis, quieren; pensar (to think) → pienso, piensas, piensa, pensamos, pensáis, piensan; empezar (to start) → empiezo, empiezas, empieza, empezamos, empezáis, empiezan; tener (to have) → tengo, tienes, tiene, tenemos, tenéis, tienen (yo form is irregular: tengo); venir (to come) → vengo, vienes, viene, venimos, venís, vienen (yo form is also irregular: vengo). Notice that tener and venir have a special yo form ending in -go in addition to the stem change.
sections.tip: Draw a 'boot' around the four forms that change (yo, tú, él/ella, ellos/ellas). The nosotros and vosotros forms keep the original stem — they fall outside the boot. For tener and venir, also remember the irregular yo forms: tengo and vengo.

examples.es: La clase empieza a las nueve.

examples.en: She has a lot of work.

exercises.prompt: What is the correct form of 'querer' (to want) for 'yo'?
exercises.explanation: The e in the stem changes to ie when stressed: quer- → quier-. Yo quiero.

exercises.prompt: Tú ___ (pensar) que el examen es difícil. (You think the exam is hard.)
exercises.hint: The stem vowel e changes to ie for tú
exercises.explanation: pensar → pens- becomes piens- when stressed: tú piensas.

exercises.prompt: How do you conjugate 'empezar' (to start) for 'ella'?
exercises.explanation: The stressed e in the stem changes to ie: empez- → empiez-. Ella empieza.

exercises.prompt: Nosotros ___ (querer) ir al cine. (We want to go to the movies.)
exercises.hint: Nosotros falls outside the 'boot' — no stem change here
exercises.explanation: The nosotros form keeps the original stem: queremos. No e→ie change.

exercises.prompt: What is the yo form of 'tener' (to have)?
exercises.explanation: Tener has an irregular yo form: tengo. The other forms follow the e→ie pattern (tienes, tiene, tienen) but yo is simply tengo.

exercises.prompt: Ellos ___ (venir) a la fiesta esta noche. (They are coming to the party tonight.)
exercises.hint: The e in the stem changes to ie for ellos — the stem is vien-
exercises.explanation: venir → ven- becomes vien- when stressed: ellos vienen.

exercises.prompt: Which sentence uses a stem-changing verb correctly?
options: Tú tienes hambre. / Tú tenes hambre.
exercises.explanation: Tener changes e→ie for tú: tienes. 'Tenes' is not a word in Spanish.

exercises.prompt: Yo ___ (empezar) a estudiar ahora. (I am starting to study now.)
exercises.hint: The e changes to ie for yo; also note the z stays as z before o
exercises.explanation: empezar → empez- + ie stem change → empiez- + o = empiezo.

sections.title: O → UE Stem Changes
sections.explanation: Another common stem-change pattern replaces the stressed 'o' in the verb stem with 'ue'. Like the e→ie verbs, this change occurs in all present-tense forms except nosotros and vosotros (the boot pattern). Key verbs: poder (to be able to/can) → puedo, puedes, puede, podemos, podéis, pueden; dormir (to sleep) → duermo, duermes, duerme, dormimos, dormís, duermen; volver (to return) → vuelvo, vuelves, vuelve, volvemos, volvéis, vuelven; costar (to cost) → cuesta (used mostly in 3rd person: cuesta, cuestan); encontrar (to find/meet) → encuentro, encuentras, encuentra, encontramos, encontráis, encuentran. Note: costar is almost always used in the 3rd person singular (cuesta) or plural (cuestan) since prices are the subject.
sections.tip: The boot pattern applies here too: yo, tú, él/ella, and ellos/ellas all get the ue change; nosotros and vosotros keep the original o. A useful trick: the ue forms are the ones you say most often in everyday conversation.

examples.en: How much does this book cost?

exercises.prompt: What is the correct form of 'poder' (to be able to) for 'yo'?
exercises.explanation: The o in the stem changes to ue when stressed: pod- → pued-. Yo puedo.

exercises.prompt: ¿Tú ___ (dormir) bien por las noches? (Do you sleep well at night?)
exercises.hint: The o in the stem changes to ue for tú
exercises.explanation: dormir → dorm- becomes duerm- when stressed: tú duermes.

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'This jacket costs fifty euros'?
exercises.explanation: Costar changes o→ue in the 3rd person singular: cuesta. 'Costa' is incorrect; 'cuestan' is plural.

exercises.prompt: Nosotros ___ (volver) a las diez. (We return at ten.)
exercises.hint: Nosotros is outside the boot — no stem change
exercises.explanation: The nosotros form keeps the original stem: volvemos. No o→ue change.

exercises.prompt: What is the correct form of 'encontrar' (to find) for 'ellos'?
exercises.explanation: The o in encontr- changes to ue: encuentran. The boot pattern applies to ellos/ellas.

exercises.prompt: Yo ___ (volver) pronto. (I will return soon. / I am returning soon.)
exercises.hint: The o changes to ue for yo; the ending is -o
exercises.explanation: volver → volv- + ue stem change → vuelv- + o = vuelvo.

exercises.prompt: Which of these correctly uses a o→ue stem-changing verb?
exercises.explanation: Poder changes o→ue for ella: puede. 'Pode' misses the stem change; 'puedes' is the tú form.

sections.title: E → I Stem Changes
sections.explanation: A third group of stem-changing verbs, all of which are -IR verbs, changes the stressed 'e' in the stem to 'i'. As with the other patterns, this change follows the boot pattern: it occurs in yo, tú, él/ella, and ellos/ellas, but NOT in nosotros or vosotros. Key verbs: pedir (to ask for/order) → pido, pides, pide, pedimos, pedís, piden; servir (to serve) → sirvo, sirves, sirve, servimos, servís, sirven; repetir (to repeat) → repito, repites, repite, repetimos, repetís, repiten; seguir (to follow/continue) → sigo, sigues, sigue, seguimos, seguís, siguen. Note: seguir drops the 'u' in the yo form (sigo, not sigueo) to maintain the hard 'g' sound. The e→i change is exclusive to -IR verbs — you will never see it in -AR or -ER verbs.
sections.tip: All e→i stem-changing verbs are -IR verbs. If you see a stem-change and the infinitive ends in -ir, it is likely e→i. The nosotros and vosotros forms keep the original e: pedimos, servimos, repetimos, seguimos.

exercises.prompt: What is the correct form of 'pedir' (to order/ask for) for 'yo'?
exercises.explanation: The e in the stem changes to i when stressed: ped- → pid-. Yo pido.

exercises.prompt: El restaurante ___ (servir) platos típicos. (The restaurant serves typical dishes.)
exercises.hint: The e changes to i; the él/ella ending is -e
exercises.explanation: servir → serv- becomes sirv- when stressed: sirve. El restaurante sirve platos típicos.

exercises.prompt: How do you conjugate 'repetir' (to repeat) for 'tú'?
exercises.explanation: The e in repit- changes to i: tú repites. 'Repetes' uses the wrong stem; 'repetimos' is the nosotros form.

exercises.prompt: Nosotros ___ (pedir) más tiempo. (We ask for more time.)
exercises.hint: Nosotros stays outside the boot — no stem change
exercises.explanation: The nosotros form keeps the original e: pedimos. No e→i change.

exercises.prompt: What is the yo form of 'seguir' (to follow)?
exercises.explanation: Seguir drops the 'u' in the yo form to keep a hard 'g' sound: sigo. 'Sigueo' is not a word; 'sigue' is the él/ella form.

exercises.prompt: Ellos ___ (repetir) las palabras nuevas. (They repeat the new words.)
exercises.hint: The e changes to i for ellos; the ending is -en
exercises.explanation: repetir → repit- + i stem change → repit- + en = repiten.

exercises.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'servir' (to serve)?
options: Ellas sirven la cena a las ocho.
Ellas serven la cena a las ocho.
Ellas servimos la cena a las ocho.
exercises.explanation: The e→i change applies to ellas: sirven. 'Serven' misses the stem change; 'servimos' is the nosotros form.

exercises.prompt: ¿Tú ___ (seguir) estudiando español? (Are you continuing to study Spanish?)
exercises.hint: The e changes to i for tú; note seguir drops the u except in yo
exercises.explanation: seguir → segu- + i stem change → sigu- + es = sigues. (The 'u' stays to keep the hard 'g' before e.)
